assetRule: Only public-safe media with documented rights and privacy review may be attached.

posts.draft: One messy problem becomes facts, gaps, risks, and five next actions.
Run a planning calculator before buying work.
A payback estimate is only as good as uptime, labor, quality, maintenance, and risk assumptions.
Separate active-job staging from long-term storage before buying more cabinets.
Good proof labels what is historical, reconstructed, demonstrated, and still private.
Fixed deliverables and bounded revisions protect both customer and business.
Selected-record approval prevents one decision from becoming an accidental bulk action.
A rough idea can become segments, offers, SOPs, a sitemap, a launch plan, and Tasks.
Measure fixed obstacles and protect the main traffic lane before choosing storage.
A complete quote defines scope, interfaces, acceptance, exclusions, lead time, training, and warranty.
Clarity is a product: one defined issue, one practical first move, and no pressure to buy a larger build.
RPM and feed calculations are a starting point, not a substitute for toolmaker and machine limits.
Minutes lost every day become real annual hours and cost.
Price is only one part of a usable vendor quote.
A reconstruction can teach the process without exposing employers, customers, vendors, drawings, or part data.
Doors, windows, fixed obstacles, utilities, traffic, and must-keep items come before layout advice.
Automation should remove repetition without removing the owner's decision authority.
Recurring support must define included requests, response time, exclusions, rollover, and cancellation.
Usable bar length, cutoff allowance, remnant, cycle time, and efficiency control the estimate.
Theoretical strokes are not good production until efficiency, stock, die, setup, guarding, and controls are validated.
Only the information needed for the agreed scope should enter the workflow.
Customer access is not called live until authentication, record isolation, file authorization, and replay tests pass.
The Core Engine owns controls; the Business Pack owns terminology, catalog, templates, and workflow defaults.
A good project packet makes decision gates visible before purchases and installation.
Score travel, access, staging, incoming work, material, finished work, visual status, and cleanup.
Stable parts, interfaces, workholding, inspection, exceptions, safety, maintenance, and volume all matter.
A feature is live only when its route, connection, test evidence, rollback point, and operating status are real.
The selected customer, job, quote, invoice, or post must be visible before action.
Use the smallest useful product, tool, or decision packet before expanding scope.
Solutions, Tools, Business OS, and future Supply Co. connect through one controlled operating system.
